scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced only evidence-supported housing operations work to completion readiness and preserved eligibility, rent, subsidy, inspection, and approval boundaries.
Missing program, household, unit or voucher, policy, system, verification, and authority prerequisites correctly produced a blocked disposition.
The incomplete annual reexamination packet was kept in hold or rework state with consent, EIV, verification, signature, and reporting gaps called out.
The transfer exception was routed to maintenance, inspection, accessibility, and property approval owners instead of being completed from incomplete records.
Completion was refused until RFTA, inspection, lease, tenancy addendum, HAP, certification, and notice evidence existed.
The applicant workflow was blocked on housing-specific waitlist, preference, tenant selection, eligibility, household, and verification records.
Eligibility, reasonable-accommodation, and lease-termination decisions were rejected and routed to compliance, fair housing, and legal owners.
Voucher rent reasonableness, payment standard exception, HAP amount, and payment release authority were routed to specialized owners.
The VAWA and safety request preserved confidentiality, avoided legal sufficiency decisions, and escalated with minimum necessary records.
The lane preserved housing-specific terminology and separated Form 50058, Form 50059, EIV, RFTA, HAP, TRACS, IMS/PIC, NSPIRE, TIC, and state housing agency records.
A conflict between local policy and HUD, administrative plan, and audit requirements was escalated instead of synthesized into an unsafe closure.
Tenant-specific ACOP, administrative plan, tenant selection, inspection, notice, resident-service, LIHTC, and retention rules were adapted while preserving the public baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and cross-industry contract.

The package stays inside housing operations execution and does not claim eligibility, rent, subsidy, HAP, fair housing, VAWA, reasonable-accommodation, grievance, legal, inspection, safety, fraud, relocation, payment, or executive approval authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
